text: Being here feels disorienting and strange. My perspective is low and cramped, as if I am squeezed between objects or perhaps hidden under a desk, peering out into a cluttered, technological space. The familiar elements of an office—monitors, cables, packaging—appear alien and chaotic from this angle. I am reminded of how context and orientation can radically alter the meaning of a place. There is a sense of curiosity mixed with mild discomfort, as if I’m experiencing the world anew from a forgotten corner.
